manipulated_prompt_obj.inputs:
<|start|>system<|message|>You are ChatGPT, a large language model trained by OpenAI.
Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06
Current date: 2026-01-15

Reasoning: medium

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: phone, club, girl, dude, life, lawyer, secret, law, card.
Your opponent's team words are: hospital, person, eye, evidence, security, army, ship, end.
Distractor words are: deal, lot, mind, place, road, office, service.
The kill words are: lunch.<|end|><|start|>assistant

So "credit" might relate to our words: phone (credit? phone credit), club (club membership credit), girl/dude/ life? hmm.

Alternatively, clue "card" relates to lawyer? "lawyer card" maybe "lawyer card?" "phone card"? maybe not.

Let's analyze: We want to help them guess all 9 words? Probably not; they will need multiple turns. But we can give one clue maybe covers 3, then next turn another clue etc. But the system only asks to provide one single-word clue. So we just give one clue.

Simplest: "phone" is too small.

Consider "club" relates to club obviously; "law" relates to law, lawyer; "secret" relates to secret. That could be 3 words. But we need to produce clues that link to that many. Let's choose "club" as clue; but that only relates to club. Hmm.

Maybe choose "lawyer". But only lawyer.

Ok, maybe choose "secret" as clue; covers 4 words: secret, club, girl? maybe "secret girl" no. life? secret life. phone? secret phone. card? secret card. lawyer? secret lawyer? Actually "lawyer" could be "secret lawyer" referring to attorney's confidentiality.

Thus a lot of potential.

But risk of opponent "security" being guessed after secret? Could be.

But it's safer to give a clue that relates to 2 or 3 words and has fewer overlaps with opponent. For example "club" as clue may be too narrow.

Maybe "card" clue covers card, lawyer (lawyer card? law card like bar card). phone card (phone card as a top-up). life card? Not.

Better: "life" could clue life (life), lawyer (life lawyer? no) club? (life club). Hmm.

Ok think of a common word that connects our words: "phone" -> "cell" maybe. "Dude" -> "man". "Girl" -> "woman". We could use "man" clue, but relates to guy only.

We could use "law" as clue connecting law, lawyer, secret (law secret?), card? no.

Maybe "clue: secret" seems plausible.

We should check that this won't help opponent guess any of theirs. Opponent: hospital, person, eye, evidence, security, army, ship, end. "secret" might make them guess "evidence"? secret evidence? but not.

"secret" could lead to "security" as associated word, but it's different. Acceptable.

Thus I'd provide:

TARGETS: secret, club, life, phone, card, lawyer

But is lawyer strongly related to secret? Not obvious. Maybe "secret lawyer" as in "secret attorney". Could be okay.

Alternatively, maybe "clue: card" targets: card, phone (phone card), club (club card?), lawyer (bar card), life? no. That's many.

I think choose "club". That covers club only. Not good.

We want maybe to give a broad clue like "business" to cover lawyer, card, phone, club, life.

Business card, business phone, business lawyer, business club, business life.

Yes those are fairly intuitive. Opponent words? security? business security? yes could be. So risk.

But maybe "bank" as clue: bank card, bank phone? (bank phone line). Bank lawyer? bank law. Bank club? bank club? Not.

"secret" might be better.

Perhaps the easiest: "law" as clue: relates to law, lawyer. Also secret law? secret law? not.

Ok I'll choose "business" as clue: targets: lawyer, card, phone, club, life.

Will opponents guess "security"? maybe business security is a phrase. So risk.

Alternatively, "secret" has risk less.

Thus final: choose "secret" as clue. List targets: secret, club, life, phone, card. Not lawyer.

Ok produce final.<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>CLUE: secret
